sourceTitle: Automate work with repeating database templates

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using a repeating database template in Notion?

options.text: It automatically creates new database entries on a schedule you define.
options.explanation: Repeating templates generate entries automatically at the chosen frequency, removing the need to manually create them each time.

options.text: It locks the page so no one can edit it.
options.explanation: Repeating templates do not affect edit permissions; they only automate creation.

options.text: It syncs the database with external calendars.
options.explanation: While you can schedule meeting notes, the template itself does not sync with external calendars.

options.text: It converts a database into a spreadsheet.
options.explanation: Repeating templates keep the data in a Notion database; they do not change its format.

questions.question: Which property will a page created by a repeating template show as its creator?

options.text: The user who last edited the page
options.explanation: The creator is not the last editor; it's set by the automation.

options.text: Notion automation
options.explanation: Repeating templates are generated by Notion’s automation, so the Created By field shows "Notion automation".

options.text: The owner of the workspace
options.explanation: Ownership does not change; the automation is listed as the creator.

options.text: The user who initially set up the template
options.explanation: Even though a user configures the template, the Created By field reflects the automation.

questions.question: When creating a repeating template, where do you find the option to set its repeat frequency?

options.text: In the page’s sidebar under "Properties"
options.explanation: Properties control page fields, not template repetition.

options.text: In the three‑dot menu ("…") next to the template name in the New‑template dropdown
options.explanation: The repeat option appears after clicking the ellipsis next to the template in the dropdown.

options.text: In the workspace settings under "Automation"
options.explanation: Workspace settings manage global automation, not individual template repeat settings.

options.text: In the Notion mobile app only
options.explanation: Repeating templates can be set from the desktop/web interface, not exclusive to mobile.

questions.question: If you want a template to generate an entry every 2 months, which of the following steps is required?

options.text: Select "Custom" in the repeat menu and set the interval to 2 months
options.explanation: Choosing Custom lets you define a 2‑month interval, which will produce items every two months.

options.text: Choose "Yearly" and then edit the date manually each time
options.explanation: Yearly repeats once a year; it won’t create a 2‑month cadence.

options.text: Set the template to repeat "Every week" and rely on filters to show only bi‑monthly items
options.explanation: Weekly repetition creates too many items; filters can’t change the creation schedule.

options.text: Enable "Duplicate after completion" in the template options
options.explanation: Duplicating after completion is a different feature; it doesn’t set a periodic schedule.

questions.question: Which scenario best demonstrates a misuse of repeating templates according to the guide?

options.text: Creating a weekly status update doc with pre‑filled headers
options.explanation: This is a recommended use: automating regular docs.

options.text: Generating a one‑time project kickoff page
options.explanation: Repeating templates are intended for recurring items; a one‑time page doesn’t need repetition.

options.text: Scheduling meeting notes for a daily stand‑up
options.explanation: Daily stand‑ups are a classic use case for repeating templates.

options.text: Automating quarterly OKR planning pages
options.explanation: Quarterly OKR pages are a perfect example of recurring content.

questions.question: How can you stop a repeating template from creating new entries?

options.text: Delete the template or toggle the "Repeat" switch off in its options
options.explanation: Both deleting the template or turning off Repeat will halt further automatic creation.

options.text: Rename the template to include the word "Stop"
options.explanation: Renaming does not affect the repeat setting.

options.text: Archive the database that contains the template
options.explanation: Archiving the database removes access but doesn’t specifically stop the repeat function.

options.text: Change the page’s "Created By" property to a user name
options.explanation: The Created By property is read‑only for automation; altering it won’t stop repeats.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why repeating templates can reduce management overhead?

options.text: They eliminate the need for any human interaction with the database.
options.explanation: Human interaction is still required to fill out or complete the generated pages.

options.text: They automatically generate standardized pages on schedule, so team members no longer need to manually create repetitive items.
options.explanation: By auto‑creating standardized entries, the team saves time and reduces the chance of missed tasks.

options.text: They sync all database entries to external project‑management tools, removing the need for Notion.
options.explanation: Syncing is not a feature of repeating templates.

options.text: They convert tasks into calendar events, which automatically sends reminders.
options.explanation: While templates can be used for meeting notes, they don’t directly create calendar events or reminders.

questions.question: A team wants a template that creates a page every Tuesday and Thursday. Which setting should they choose?

options.text: Select "Weekly" and then choose Tuesday and Thursday in the custom days picker
options.explanation: The custom repeat option lets you pick specific weekdays within a weekly schedule.

options.text: Select "Every 2 days" in the custom frequency
options.explanation: Every 2 days would produce a different pattern (e.g., Mon‑Wed‑Fri) not fixed to Tuesday/Thursday.

options.text: Choose "Monthly" and set the day to 2 and 4
options.explanation: Monthly repeats on calendar dates, not specific weekdays.

options.text: Use the "Yearly" option and manually edit the dates each year
options.explanation: Yearly is far too infrequent for a twice‑weekly schedule.
